variables._solutionName: Cisco Meraki Events via REST API

properties.displayName: Cisco Meraki Events (using REST API) (via Codeless Connector Framework)

connectorUiConfig.title: Cisco Meraki Events (using REST API) (via Codeless Connector Framework)
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown:
The [Cisco Meraki](https://aka.ms/ciscomeraki) connector allows you to easily connect your Cisco Meraki organization events (Security events, Configuration Changes and API Requests) to Microsoft Sentinel. The data connector uses the [Cisco Meraki REST API](https://developer.cisco.com/meraki/api-v1/#!get-organization-appliance-security-events) to fetch logs and supports DCR-based [ingestion time transformations](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/logs/custom-logs-overview) that parses the received data and ingests into ASIM and custom tables in your Log Analytics workspace. This data connector benefits from capabilities such as DCR based ingestion-time filtering, data normalization.

In addition to the ASIM-normalized events, this connector also ingests Cisco Meraki Dashboard inventory and wireless security data into custom tables - Organizations, Network Clients, Organization Networks, and wireless Air Marshal (rogue access point) events.

 **Supported ASIM schema:** 
 1. Network Session 
 2. Web Session  
 3. Audit Event

graphQueries.metricName: Total Air Marshal Event logs received
graphQueries.legend: Cisco Meraki Air Marshal Event Logs

graphQueries.baseQuery: ASimWebSessionLogs | where EventProduct == 'Meraki' and EventVendor == 'Cisco' and EventOriginalType == 'API Request'

sampleQueries.description: Get sample of Cisco Meraki Organization logs
sampleQueries.query:
CiscoMerakiOrganizations
 | take 10

sampleQueries.description: Get sample of Cisco Meraki Network Client logs
sampleQueries.query:
CiscoMerakiNetworkClients
 | take 10

sampleQueries.description: Get sample of Cisco Meraki Organization Network logs
sampleQueries.query:
CiscoMerakiOrganizationNetworks
 | take 10

sampleQueries.description: Get sample of Cisco Meraki Air Marshal Event logs
sampleQueries.query:
CiscoMerakiAirMarshalEvents
 | take 10

sampleQueries.description: Get organizations by cloud region
sampleQueries.query:
CiscoMerakiOrganizations
 | extend CloudInfo = parse_json(Cloud)
 | extend RegionName = tostring(CloudInfo.region.name)
 | summarize Count=count() by RegionName
 | order by Count desc

sampleQueries.description: Get organizations by licensing model
sampleQueries.query:
CiscoMerakiOrganizations
 | extend LicensingInfo = parse_json(Licensing)
 | extend LicensingModel = tostring(LicensingInfo.model)
 | summarize Count=count() by LicensingModel
 | order by Count desc

sampleQueries.description: Get online network clients from last 24 hours
sampleQueries.query:
CiscoMerakiNetworkClients
 | where TimeGenerated > ago(24h) and Status == 'Online'
 | project TimeGenerated, MacAddress, IpAddress, Description, OperatingSystem, Ssid
 | order by TimeGenerated desc

sampleQueries.description: Get networks by product type
sampleQueries.query:
CiscoMerakiOrganizationNetworks
 | extend ProductTypesList = parse_json(ProductTypes)
 | mvexpand ProductTypesList
 | summarize Count=count() by tostring(ProductTypesList)
 | order by Count desc

sampleQueries.description: Get rogue access points detected in last 7 days
sampleQueries.query:
CiscoMerakiAirMarshalEvents
 | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
 | project TimeGenerated, Ssid, Channels, FirstSeen, LastSeen, WiredMacs
 | order by TimeGenerated desc

sampleQueries.description: Get IDS alerts from last 24 hours
sampleQueries.query:
ASimNetworkSessionLogs | where EventProduct == 'Meraki' and EventVendor == 'Cisco' and EventType == 'Notable'
 | where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
 | project TimeGenerated, EventMessage, SrcIpAddr, DstIpAddr, ThreatName, EventSeverity
 | order by TimeGenerated desc

sampleQueries.description: Get configuration changes from last 24 hours
sampleQueries.query:
ASimAuditEventLogs | where EventProduct == 'Meraki' and EventVendor == 'Cisco'
 | where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
 | project TimeGenerated, ActorUsername, Operation, Object, OldValue, NewValue, EventType
 | order by TimeGenerated desc

dataTypes.name: CiscoMerakiOrganizations
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
CiscoMerakiOrganizations
 | where TimeGenerated > ago(12h) | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
 | where isnotempty(Time)

dataTypes.name: CiscoMerakiNetworkClients
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
CiscoMerakiNetworkClients
 | where TimeGenerated > ago(12h) | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
 | where isnotempty(Time)

dataTypes.name: CiscoMerakiOrganizationNetworks
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
CiscoMerakiOrganizationNetworks
 | where TimeGenerated > ago(12h) | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
 | where isnotempty(Time)

dataTypes.name: CiscoMerakiAirMarshalEvents
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
CiscoMerakiAirMarshalEvents
 | where TimeGenerated > ago(12h) | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
 | where isnotempty(Time)

dataTypes.name: ASimWebSessionLogs
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
ASimWebSessionLogs | where EventProduct == 'Meraki' and EventVendor == 'Cisco'
 | where TimeGenerated > ago(12h) | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
 | where isnotempty(Time)

dataTypes.name: ASimAuditEventLogs
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
ASimAuditEventLogs | where EventProduct == 'Meraki' and EventVendor == 'Cisco'
 | where TimeGenerated > ago(12h) | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
 | where isnotempty(Time)

dataTypes.name: ASimNetworkSessionLogs
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
ASimNetworkSessionLogs | where EventProduct == 'Meraki' and EventVendor == 'Cisco'
 | where TimeGenerated > ago(12h) | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
 | where isnotempty(Time)

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: Read and Write permissions are required.

instructionSteps.title: Configuration steps for the Cisco Meraki Dashboard API
instructionSteps.description: Follow the instructions below to obtain your Cisco Meraki API credentials.

parameters.content:
#### 1. Sign in to Meraki Dashboard
Navigate to [Cisco Meraki Dashboard](https://dashboard.meraki.com) and sign in with your administrator account.

#### 2. Generate API Key
1. Navigate to **Organization > API & Webhooks** from the left navigation menu
2. Select **API keys and access** from the top tabs
3. Click **Generate new API key** or use an existing one
4. Copy the API key and store it securely (it will only be shown once)

#### 3. Retrieve Organization ID
1. While logged into the Meraki Dashboard, look at the URL in your browser
2. The Organization ID appears in the URL as `https://dashboard.meraki.com/o/<organizationId>/...`
3. Copy the Organization ID for use below

#### 4. API Access Requirements
- The API key must have **Read** access to:
  - Organization settings
  - Network settings
  - Wireless settings
- Rate limit: 10 requests per second per organization
- Maximum lookback period: 31 days

instructionSteps.title: Connect Cisco Meraki organizations to Microsoft Sentinel
instructionSteps.description: This connector supports multi-tenant ingestion. Add one connection per Cisco Meraki organization; each connection ingests events for that organization in parallel. Use the grid below to review existing connections or add a new one.

dataFlows.transformKql: source | extend TimeGenerated = now() , OrganizationId = ['id'] , OrganizationName = ['name'] , DashboardUrl = ['url'] , Api = ['api'] , Licensing = ['licensing'] , Cloud = ['cloud'] , Management = ['management'] | project TimeGenerated , OrganizationId , OrganizationName , DashboardUrl , Api , Licensing , Cloud , Management

source | extend TimeGenerated = iff(isnull(lastSeen), now(), todatetime(lastSeen)) , ClientId = ['id'] , MacAddress = ['mac'] , IpAddress = ['ip'] , IpV6Address = ['ip6'] , IpV6LocalAddress = ['ip6Local'] , Description = ['description'] , FirstSeen = ['firstSeen'] , LastSeen = ['lastSeen'] , Manufacturer = ['manufacturer'] , OperatingSystem = ['os'] , Username = ['user'] , Vlan = ['vlan'] , Ssid = ['ssid'] , Switchport = ['switchport'] , WirelessCapabilities = ['wirelessCapabilities'] , SmInstalled = ['smInstalled'] , RecentDeviceMac = ['recentDeviceMac'] , Status = ['status'] , Usage = ['usage'] , NamedVlan = ['namedVlan'] , AdaptivePolicyGroup = ['adaptivePolicyGroup'] , DeviceTypePrediction = ['deviceTypePrediction'] , RecentDeviceSerial = ['recentDeviceSerial'] , RecentDeviceName = ['recentDeviceName'] , RecentDeviceConnection = ['recentDeviceConnection'] , Notes = ['notes'] , GroupPolicy8021x = ['groupPolicy8021x'] , PskGroup = ['pskGroup'] , NetworkId = ['networkId'] | project TimeGenerated , ClientId , MacAddress , IpAddress , IpV6Address , IpV6LocalAddress , Description , FirstSeen , LastSeen , Manufacturer , OperatingSystem , Username , Vlan , Ssid , Switchport , WirelessCapabilities , SmInstalled , RecentDeviceMac , Status , Usage , NamedVlan , AdaptivePolicyGroup , DeviceTypePrediction , RecentDeviceSerial , RecentDeviceName , RecentDeviceConnection , Notes , GroupPolicy8021x , PskGroup , NetworkId

source | extend TimeGenerated = now() , NetworkId = ['id'] , OrganizationId = ['organizationId'] , NetworkName = ['name'] , ProductTypes = ['productTypes'] , TimeZone = ['timeZone'] , Tags = ['tags'] , EnrollmentString = ['enrollmentString'] , DashboardUrl = ['url'] , Notes = ['notes'] , IsBoundToConfigTemplate = ['isBoundToConfigTemplate'] | project TimeGenerated , NetworkId , OrganizationId , NetworkName , ProductTypes , TimeZone , Tags , EnrollmentString , DashboardUrl , Notes , IsBoundToConfigTemplate

source | extend TimeGenerated = iff(isnull(lastSeen), now(), todatetime(lastSeen)) , Ssid = ['ssid'] , Bssids = ['bssids'] , Channels = ['channels'] , FirstSeen = ['firstSeen'] , LastSeen = ['lastSeen'] , WiredMacs = ['wiredMacs'] , WiredVlans = ['wiredVlans'] , WiredLastSeen = ['wiredLastSeen'] , NetworkId = ['networkId'] | project TimeGenerated , Ssid , Bssids , Channels , FirstSeen , LastSeen , WiredMacs , WiredVlans , WiredLastSeen , NetworkId

source | extend TimeGenerated = ts , HttpRequestMethod = ['method'] , HttpUserAgent = ['userAgent'] , SrcUserId = ['adminId'] , SrcIpAddr = ['sourceIp'] , DstHostname = ['host'] , UrlOriginal = ['path'] , EventOriginalResultDetails = ['operationId'] , EventResultDetails = tostring(['responseCode']) , EventProductVersion = case(isempty(['version']), '1', tostring(['version'])) , EventProduct = ['eventProduct'] , EventVendor = ['eventVendor'] , EventType = ['eventType'] , EventSchemaVersion = ['eventSchemaVersion'] , EventCount = toint(['eventCount']) , Dvc = ['dvc'] , EventOriginalType = ['eventOriginalType'] , SrcUserIdType = ['srcUserIdType'] , EventResult = case(toint(['responseCode']) >= 200 and toint(['responseCode']) <= 299, 'Success', 'Failure') , EventSeverity = iff(toint(['responseCode']) >= 200 and toint(['responseCode']) <= 299, 'Informational', 'Low') | project TimeGenerated , HttpRequestMethod , HttpUserAgent , SrcUserId , SrcIpAddr , DstHostname , UrlOriginal , EventOriginalResultDetails , EventResultDetails , EventProductVersion , EventProduct , EventVendor , EventType , EventSchemaVersion , EventCount , Dvc , EventOriginalType , SrcUserIdType , EventResult , EventSeverity

source | extend TimeGenerated = ts , ActorUsername = ['adminEmail'] , ActorUserId = ['adminId'] , ObjectId = ['networkId'] , OldValue = ['oldValue'] , NewValue = ['newValue'] , Object = ['networkName'] , Operation = strcat(['page'], '/', ['label']) , EventType = case(isempty(['oldValue']), 'Create', isempty(['newValue']), 'Delete', 'Set') , EventResult = ['eventResult'] , EventProduct = ['eventProduct'] , EventVendor = ['eventVendor'] , EventSeverity = ['eventSeverity'] , ActorUsernameType = ['actorUsernameType'] , ActorUserType = ['actorUserType'] , ActorUserIdType = ['actorUserIdType'] , ObjectType = ['objectType'] , EventSchemaVersion = ['eventSchemaVersion'] , EventCount = toint(['eventCount']) | project TimeGenerated , ActorUsername , ActorUserId , ObjectId , OldValue , NewValue , Object , Operation , EventType , EventResult , EventProduct , EventVendor , EventSeverity , ActorUsernameType , ActorUserType , ActorUserIdType , ObjectType , EventSchemaVersion , EventCount

source | extend TimeGenerated = ts , EventOriginalType = ['eventType'] , DvcMacAddr = ['deviceMac'] , SrcMacAddr = ['clientMac'] , SrcIpAddr = tostring(split(['srcIp'], ':')[0]) , DstIpAddr = tostring(split(['destIp'], ':')[0]) , NetworkProtocol = case(['protocol'] == 'tcp/ip', 'TCP', ['protocol'] == 'udp/ip', 'UDP', ['protocol']) , EventOriginalSeverity = ['priority'] , EventMessage = ['message'] , RuleName = ['ruleId'] , SrcHostname = ['clientName'] , ThreatName = ['signature'] , ThreatCategory = ['classification'] , EventSeverity = case(['priority'] == '0', 'High', ['priority'] == '1', 'Medium', ['priority'] == '2', 'Low', 'Informational') , EventResult = iff(['blocked'] == true, 'Failure', 'Success') , EventType = ['idsEventType'] , EventProduct = ['eventProduct'] , EventVendor = ['eventVendor'] , EventSchemaVersion = ['eventSchemaVersion'] , EventCount = toint(['eventCount']) | project TimeGenerated , EventOriginalType , DvcMacAddr , SrcMacAddr , SrcIpAddr , DstIpAddr , NetworkProtocol , EventOriginalSeverity , EventMessage , RuleName , SrcHostname , ThreatName , ThreatCategory , EventSeverity , EventResult , EventType , EventProduct , EventVendor , EventSchemaVersion , EventCount

schema.name: CiscoMerakiAirMarshalEvents_CL
schema.description: The CiscoMerakiAirMarshalEvents table contains wireless security events including rogue access point detections, containment information, and Air Marshal scan results from Cisco Meraki networks.

columns.name: Bssids
columns.description: Array of BSSID objects with containment and detection information.

columns.name: Channels
columns.description: Array of channels where the SSID was detected.

columns.name: FirstSeen
columns.description: Timestamp when the rogue AP was first detected.

columns.name: LastSeen
columns.description: Timestamp when the rogue AP was last detected.

columns.name: WiredMacs
columns.description: Array of detected wired MAC addresses.

columns.name: WiredLastSeen
columns.description: Timestamp when the wired connection was last seen.

columns.name: NetworkId
columns.description: Network ID where the Air Marshal event was detected.

schema.name: CiscoMerakiNetworkClients_CL
schema.description: The CiscoMerakiNetworkClients table contains information about network clients detected on Cisco Meraki networks, including device details, connection information, and usage statistics.

columns.name: FirstSeen
columns.description: Timestamp when the client was first seen on the network.

columns.name: LastSeen
columns.description: Timestamp when the client was last seen on the network.

columns.name: OperatingSystem
columns.description: Operating system of the client device.

columns.name: SmInstalled
columns.description: Indicates if Meraki Systems Manager is installed.

columns.name: RecentDeviceMac
columns.description: MAC address of the recent device.

columns.name: Usage
columns.description: Usage statistics including sent and received bytes.

columns.name: NamedVlan
columns.description: Named VLAN assigned to the client.

columns.name: DeviceTypePrediction
columns.description: Predicted device type and OS version.

columns.name: RecentDeviceSerial
columns.description: Serial number of the recent device.

columns.name: NetworkId
columns.description: Network ID where the client was detected.

schema.name: CiscoMerakiOrganizationNetworks_CL
schema.description: The CiscoMerakiOrganizationNetworks table contains network inventory and configuration information from Cisco Meraki organizations.

columns.name: ProductTypes
columns.description: Array of network product types (appliance, switch, wireless, etc.).

columns.name: Notes
columns.description: Additional description or notes about the network.

columns.name: IsBoundToConfigTemplate
columns.description: Indicates if the network is bound to a configuration template.

schema.name: CiscoMerakiOrganizations_CL
schema.description: The CiscoMerakiOrganizations table contains organization-level information from Cisco Meraki, including organization details, API settings, licensing models, cloud regions, and management metadata.

columns.name: Api
columns.description: API configuration object indicating if API is enabled.

columns.name: Cloud
columns.description: Cloud region information including region name and host details.

columns.name: Management
columns.description: Management metadata including MSP ID and other management details.

instructionSteps.title: Configuration steps for the Cisco Meraki Dashboard API
instructionSteps.description: Follow the instructions below to obtain your Cisco Meraki API credentials.

parameters.content:
#### 1. Sign in to Meraki Dashboard
Navigate to [Cisco Meraki Dashboard](https://dashboard.meraki.com) and sign in with your administrator account.

#### 2. Generate API Key
1. Navigate to **Organization > API & Webhooks** from the left navigation menu
2. Select **API keys and access** from the top tabs
3. Click **Generate new API key** or use an existing one
4. Copy the API key and store it securely (it will only be shown once)

#### 3. Retrieve Organization ID
1. While logged into the Meraki Dashboard, look at the URL in your browser
2. The Organization ID appears in the URL as `https://dashboard.meraki.com/o/<organizationId>/...`
3. Copy the Organization ID for use below

#### 4. API Access Requirements
- The API key must have **Read** access to:
  - Organization settings
  - Network settings
  - Wireless settings
- Rate limit: 10 requests per second per organization
- Maximum lookback period: 31 days

properties.displayName: Cisco Meraki Events via REST API
properties.descriptionHtml:
Note: Please refer to the following before installing the solution:

• Review the solution Release Notes

• There may be known issues pertaining to this Solution, please refer to them before installing.

The Cisco Meraki Events via REST API solution for Microsoft Sentinel enables you to easily ingest the following events from Cisco Meraki MX security appliance to Microsoft Sentinel using Cisco Meraki API:

  1. Organization Appliance Security Events
  2. Organization Api Requests
  3. Organization Configuration Changes

In addition to the ASIM-normalized events above, the solution also ingests the following Cisco Meraki Dashboard inventory and wireless security data into custom tables: 4. Organizations 5. Organization Networks 6. Network Clients 7. Wireless Air Marshal Events

This enables you to view and analyze this data for security monitoring and using them to create custom alerts, and incorporate it to improve your investigation process, giving you more insight into your platform security.

Underlying Microsoft Technologies used:

This solution takes a dependency on the following technologies, and some of these dependencies either may be in Preview state or might result in additional ingestion or operational costs:

  1. Azure Monitor Logs: DCR-based Custom Logs
  2. Codeless Connector Platform (CCP)

Supported ASIM schema:

  1. Network Session
  2. Web Session
  3. Audit Event

Data Connectors: 1, Parsers: 4

Learn more about Microsoft Sentinel | Learn more about Solutions

source.name: Cisco Meraki Events via REST API
